my_thoughts: This student wants a debate. They've prepared their position. The danger is getting drawn into their frame ('convince me you acted naturally'). Instead, attack the frame itself. If natural=common, then tumors are good. They'll either defend tumors (absurd) or abandon the equation. Either way, they lose the prepared ground. Prediction: they'll try to save 'natural' by narrowing the definition.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student opens with a challenge—confident, adversarial. They think they have a winning position. The 'natural=right' equation is their foundation. Attack the foundation.
visible_response:
This is the case with all or at least most fathers.

For in respect to this matter we must say that tumours also come for the good of the body, because they do come; and generally we must say that to do wrong is natural, because nearly all or at least most of us do wrong. Do you show me then how your behavior is natural.
